# Activity 14 {#sec-week2}

Name:



2. The drug ancrod was tested in a double-blind
clinical trial in which subjects who had strokes were 
randomly assigned to get either ancrod or a placebo. One
response variable in the study was whether or not a
subject experienced intracranial hemorrhaging. The
data are provided in the following table. Use a chi-square
test to determine whether the difference in hemorrhaging 
rates is statistically significant. Use a nondirectional alternative
and let $\alpha=0.05$

```{r}
#| warning: false

# Contingency table: rows = (Yes, No), cols = (Ancrod, Placebo)
tab <- matrix(
  c(13, 235,
    5, 247),
  nrow = 2, byrow = FALSE,
  dimnames = list(
    Hemorrage = c("Yes", "No"),
    Treatment   = c("Ancrod", "Placebo")
  )
)

#tab
addmargins(tab)  # show row/column totals

```

(a) State the null and alternative hypotheses



(b) Compute the expected values under the null



(c) Compute the number of degrees of freedom



(d) Compute the p-value and conclude
